reviews.review_description: The app is put together well and is easy to use. It's not all that difficult, definitely worth a try if you like solving puzzles.

developer_response.body:
Hi JimBatman, thx for taking the time to provide feedback. I'm happy to hear you like it.
I will do my best to repay you with future updates :-)

reviews.review_description:
It would probably be quite nice if it worked. It could do with some sort of instructions on how to move the grid in where I presume the puzzle is meant to be built, if its possible to move it. 
It says sitting, but if I'm sitting down, the grid is way above my head and out of reach. I can pull the puzzle pieces down in front of me, but the grid won't budge and pointing at the setting wheel and clicking doesn't do anything either.
So, since I'm not able to stand very well atm, I can't really test it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Andulacka, thx for taking the time to provide feedback. Sorry about your bad experience. The game is currently a pre-release, and I agree that instructions are lacking - I will improve this in future updates.
For now, please be advised that the horizontal bar below the puzzle grid is grabbable, so you can use it to position the puzzle in any place in space that you like.
Feel free to expand on your feedback if other issues come up.
Happy puzzling! :-)

reviews.review_description: I like it. I found it easy but it maybe just me. It is like a quick puzzle and it was fun. The hardest part is to remember the pieces. Graphics looks weird sometimes... I see a crooked lines due to low resolution. Overall, I ma pretty impress. The way the game is made, it should be easy to generate puzzles. I would advise to make different level based on how many solutions are possible for the same puzzle. Also I wish more music. And different backgrounds than just stars. For example, add planets, comets, etc...
Try it you won't  regret playing  this puzzles

Interesting way to do puzzles.  I like the challenge. However, there should be different environments and maybe actual levels for degree of difficulty. I would also rather use my hands than the controllers.
